quality_notes: Add vulnerability: mention a mistake, failure, or lesson learned. Try 'I was wrong', 'took me years to learn', or 'finally understood'.
Add more relevant emojis to improve scanability and engagement (target: 4 to 10).
Include one fenced visual block that matches the planned diagram type.
Keep the post in the LinkedIn sweet spot: roughly 140 to 340 words.

post_text:
Typefaces fell into chaos when I searched my own name in ChatGPT last week. The results were a mix of outdated articles and irrelevant links. I realized that public signals, such as our online presence, play a significant role in professional discovery. This uncomfortable truth hit me - my professional brand was not accurately represented online.

I found that my LinkedIn profile was not showcasing my skills and expertise effectively, and my website was not easily discoverable. This was a wake-up call for me to take control of my online presence.

Here are the steps I'm taking to improve:

This experience changed my perspective on the importance of curating a professional online presence.

💬 How do you ensure your professional brand is accurately represented online? #PersonalBranding #ProfessionalDiscovery #OnlinePresence #CareerDevelopment #DigitalFootprint

  @keyframes pu{0%,100%{opacity:1}50%{opacity:0.55}}
  .fi{animation:fd .5s ease-out both}
  .flow{stroke-dasharray:8 4;animation:fr 1.4s linear infinite}
  .pu{animation:pu 2.2s ease-in-out infinite}
